lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਸਬਦੀ ਸਚੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਸਚਾ ਗਹਿਰ ਗੰਭੀਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: gurasabadee sach paaeaa sachaa gahir ganbheer |
lines.english: Through the Word of the Guru's Shabad, I have found the True, Profound and Unfathomable Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਸਾਲਾਹੀ ਸਦਾ ਸਦਾ ਤਨੁ ਮਨੁ ਸਉਪਿ ਸਰੀਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: har saalaahee sadaa sadaa tan man saup sareer |
lines.english: Praise the Lord, forever and ever; dedicate your body and mind to Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਿ ਤਨਿ ਹਿਰਦੈ ਰਵਿ ਰਹਿਆ ਹਰਿ ਹੀਰਾ ਹੀਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: man tan hiradai rav rahiaa har heeraa heer |
lines.english: The Lord, the jewel of jewels, is permeating my mind, body and heart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨਮ ਮਰਣ ਕਾ ਦੁਖੁ ਗਇਆ ਫਿਰਿ ਪਵੈ ਨ ਫੀਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: janam maran kaa dukh geaa fir pavai na feer |
lines.english: The pains of birth and death are gone, and I shall never again be consigned to the cycle of reincarnation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Salok, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮੁ ਸਲਾਹਿ ਤੂ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਣੀ ਗਹੀਰੁ ॥੧੦॥
lines.translit: naanak naam salaeh too har gunee gaheer |10|
lines.english: O Nanak, praise the Naam, the Name of the Lord, the ocean of excellence. ||10||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਇਹੁ ਤਨੁ ਜਾਲਿ ਜਿਨਿ ਜਲਿਐ ਨਾਮੁ ਵਿਸਾਰਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: naanak ihu tan jaal jin jaliai naam visaariaa |
lines.english: O Nanak, burn this body; this burnt body has forgotten the Naam, the Name of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉਦੀ ਜਾਇ ਪਰਾਲਿ ਪਿਛੈ ਹਥੁ ਨ ਅੰਬੜੈ ਤਿਤੁ ਨਿਵੰਧੈ ਤਾਲਿ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: paudee jaae paraal pichhai hath na anbarrai tith nivandhai taal |1|
lines.english: The dirt is piling up, and in the world hereafter, your hand shall not be able to reach down into this stagnant pond to clean it out. ||1||

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਮਨ ਕੇ ਕੰਮ ਫਿਟਿਆ ਗਣਤ ਨ ਆਵਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: naanak man ke kam fittiaa ganat na aavahee |
lines.english: O Nanak, wicked are the uncountable actions of the mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਤੀ ਲਹਾ ਸਹੰਮ ਜਾ ਬਖਸੇ ਤਾ ਧਕਾ ਨਹੀ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: kitee lahaa saham jaa bakhase taa dhakaa nahee |2|
lines.english: They bring terrible and painful retributions, but if the Lord forgives me, then I will be spared this punishment. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਦਾ ਨਿਹਚਲੁ ਰਵਿ ਰਹਿਆ ਸੋ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਸੁਜਾਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: sadaa nihachal rav rahiaa so purakh sujaan |
lines.english: Forever unmoving and unchanging, permeating and pervading everywhere, He is the All-knowing Primal Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚਾ ਅਮਰੁ ਚਲਾਇਓਨੁ ਕਰਿ ਸਚੁ ਫੁਰਮਾਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: sachaa amar chalaaeon kar sach furamaan |
lines.english: True is the Command He sends forth, and True are the Orders He issues.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦੀ ਸੇਵੀਐ ਸਚੁ ਸਬਦਿ ਨੀਸਾਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: gur parasaadee seveeai sach sabad neesaan |
lines.english: By Guru's Grace, serve Him, through the True Insignia of the Shabad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਗਮ ਅਗੋਚਰੁ ਅਲਖੁ ਹੈ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹਰਿ ਜਾਣੁ ॥੧੧॥
lines.translit: agam agochar alakh hai guramukh har jaan |11|
lines.english: He is inaccessible, unfathomable and unseen; as Gurmukh, know the Lord. ||11||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਰਾ ਥਾਟੁ ਬਣਾਇਆ ਰੰਗੁ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਮਾਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: pooraa thaatt banaaeaa rang guramat maan |
lines.english: That which He makes is perfect; through the Guru's Teachings, enjoy His Love.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਬਦਰਾ ਮਾਲ ਕਾ ਭੀਤਰਿ ਧਰਿਆ ਆਣਿ ॥
lines.translit: naanak badaraa maal kaa bheetar dhariaa aan |
lines.english: O Nanak, the bags of coins are brought in

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Salok, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਖੋਟੇ ਖਰੇ ਪਰਖੀਅਨਿ ਸਾਹਿਬ ਕੈ ਦੀਬਾਣਿ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: khotte khare parakheean saahib kai deebaan |1|
lines.english: and placed in the Court of our Lord and Master, and there, the genuine and the counterfeit are separated. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕੁ ਭਾਉ ਲਥੀ ਨਾਤਿਆ ਦੁਇ ਭਾ ਚੜੀਅਸੁ ਹੋਰ ॥
lines.translit: eik bhaau lathee naatiaa due bhaa charreeas hor |
lines.english: Some of their filth is washed off by these baths, but they only accumulate twice as much.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਵਣ ਚਲੇ ਤੀਰਥੀ ਮਨਿ ਖੋਟੈ ਤਨਿ ਚੋਰ ॥
lines.translit: naavan chale teerathee man khottai tan chor |
lines.english: They go and bathe at sacred shrines of pilgrimage, but their minds are still evil, and their bodies are thieves.

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਹਰਿ ਧੋਤੀ ਤੂਮੜੀ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਵਿਸੁ ਨਿਕੋਰ ॥
lines.translit: baahar dhotee toomarree andar vis nikor |
lines.english: Like a gourd, they may be washed off on the outside, but on the inside, they are still filled with poison.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਧ ਭਲੇ ਅਣਨਾਤਿਆ ਚੋਰ ਸਿ ਚੋਰਾ ਚੋਰ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: saadh bhale ananaatiaa chor si choraa chor |2|
lines.english: The holy man is blessed, even without such bathing, while a thief is a thief, no matter how much he bathes. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਚਲਾਇਦਾ ਜਗੁ ਧੰਧੈ ਲਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: aape hukam chalaaeidaa jag dhandhai laaeaa |
lines.english: He Himself issues His Commands, and links the people of the world to their tasks.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਿ ਆਪੇ ਹੀ ਆਪਿ ਲਾਇਅਨੁ ਗੁਰ ਤੇ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: eik aape hee aap laaeian gur te sukh paaeaa |
lines.english: He Himself joins some to Himself, and through the Guru, they find peace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਵੈ ਨੋ ਸਭ ਲੋਚਦੀ ਗੁਰਮਤੀ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: naavai no sabh lochadee guramatee paaeaa |
lines.english: Everyone longs for the Name, but it is only found through the Guru's Teachings.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਹ ਦਿਸ ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਧਾਵਦਾ ਗੁਰਿ ਠਾਕਿ ਰਹਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: deh dis ihu man dhaavadaa gur tthaak rahaaeaa |
lines.english: The mind runs around in the ten directions; the Guru holds it still.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਇ ਦੀਵੇ ਚਉਦਹ ਹਟਨਾਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: due deeve chaudah hattanaale |
lines.english: The two lamps light the fourteen markets.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Salok, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਧੁਰਿ ਲਿਖਿਆ ਮੇਟਿ ਨ ਸਕੀਐ ਜੋ ਹਰਿ ਲਿਖਿ ਪਾਇਆ ॥੧੨॥
lines.translit: dhur likhiaa mett na sakeeai jo har likh paaeaa |12|
lines.english: Your pre-ordained destiny, written by the Lord in the very beginning, cannot be erased. ||12||

lines.gurmukhi: ਖੁਲੑੇ ਹਟ ਹੋਆ ਵਾਪਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: khulae hatt hoaa vaapaar |
lines.english: The shops are open, and trading is going on;

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਰਮੁ ਦਲਾਲੁ ਪਾਏ ਨੀਸਾਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: dharam dalaal paae neesaan |
lines.english: The Righteous Judge of Dharma is the broker, who gives his sign of approval.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਪਹੁਚੈ ਸੋ ਚਲਣਹਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: jo pahuchai so chalanahaar |
lines.english: whoever comes there, is bound to depart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇਤੇ ਜੀਅ ਤੇਤੇ ਵਣਜਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: jete jeea tete vanajaare |
lines.english: There are just as many traders as there are living beings.

lines.gurmukhi: ਘਰਿ ਆਏ ਵਜੀ ਵਾਧਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: ghar aae vajee vaadhaaee |
lines.english: And when they return home, they are greeted with cheers;

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮੁ ਲਾਹਾ ਪਰਵਾਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: naanak naam laahaa paravaan |
lines.english: O Nanak, those who earn the profit of the Naam are accepted and approved.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚ ਨਾਮ ਕੀ ਮਿਲੀ ਵਡਿਆਈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: sach naam kee milee vaddiaaee |1|
lines.english: they obtain the glorious greatness of the True Name. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਧੇ ਅਕਲੀ ਬਾਹਰੇ ਮੂਰਖ ਅੰਧ ਗਿਆਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: andhe akalee baahare moorakh andh giaan |
lines.english: The blind fools have no wisdom at all; their understanding is blind.

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਤੀ ਹੋਵਨਿ ਕਾਲੀਆ ਸੁਪੇਦਾ ਸੇ ਵੰਨ ॥
lines.translit: raatee hovan kaaleea supedaa se van |
lines.english: Even when the night is dark, whatever is white retains its white color.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਿਹੁ ਬਗਾ ਤਪੈ ਘਣਾ ਕਾਲਿਆ ਕਾਲੇ ਵੰਨ ॥
lines.translit: dihu bagaa tapai ghanaa kaaliaa kaale van |
lines.english: And even when the light of day is dazzlingly bright, whatever is black retains its black color.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਦਰੀ ਬਾਹਰੇ ਕਬਹਿ ਨ ਪਾਵਹਿ ਮਾਨੁ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak nadaree baahare kabeh na paaveh maan |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, without the Lord's Grace, they will never receive honor. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਇਆ ਕੋਟੁ ਰਚਾਇਆ ਹਰਿ ਸਚੈ ਆਪੇ ॥
lines.translit: kaaeaa kott rachaaeaa har sachai aape |
lines.english: The True Lord Himself created the body-fortress.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਿ ਦੂਜੈ ਭਾਇ ਖੁਆਇਅਨੁ ਹਉਮੈ ਵਿਚਿ ਵਿਆਪੇ ॥
lines.translit: eik doojai bhaae khuaaeian haumai vich viaape |
lines.english: Some are ruined through the love of duality, engrossed in egotism.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹੁ ਮਾਨਸ ਜਨਮੁ ਦੁਲੰਭੁ ਸਾ ਮਨਮੁਖ ਸੰਤਾਪੇ ॥
lines.translit: eihu maanas janam dulanbh saa manamukh santaape |
lines.english: This human body is so difficult to obtain; the self-willed manmukhs suffer in pain.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭੁ ਜਗੁ ਖੇਲੁ ਰਚਾਇਓਨੁ ਸਭ ਵਰਤੈ ਆਪੇ ॥੧੩॥
lines.translit: sabh jag khel rachaaeon sabh varatai aape |13|
lines.english: He created the entire world for His play; He is pervading amongst all. ||13||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਆਪਿ ਬੁਝਾਏ ਸੋ ਬੁਝਸੀ ਜਿਸੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਥਾਪੇ ॥
lines.translit: jis aap bujhaae so bujhasee jis satigur thaape |
lines.english: He alone understands, whom the Lord Himself causes to understand; he is blessed by the True Guru.
